extra.message:
Confirmed multiple broken access control / IDOR vulnerabilities in the Django employee portal. The code consistently uses only @user_is_authenticated (app/decorators.py:5-19), which validates presence of a cookie-backed user but does not enforce object ownership or admin role checks. 

1) Missing admin authorization on all /admin routes. app/views/admin/urls.py:5-10 exposes admin dashboard, user lookup, delete, update, list-all-users, and analytics endpoints keyed by selected_id. Each handler on

Confirmed multiple sensitive-data exposure vulnerabilities caused by missing object-level authorization and unauthenticated/weakly authenticated data APIs in the employee portal.

1) Cross-user disclosure of work information (IDOR)
- Route: /users/<user_id>/work_info/
- URL mapping: app/views/users/urls.py:19 includes work_info routes beneath /users/<user_id>/work_info/
- Sink: app/views/users/work_info/views.py:11-16
  * user = User.objects.get(id=user_id) at line 12 uses the path parameter dir

Confirmed CSRF vulnerabilities in authenticated, state-changing endpoints of the Django web application. Global protection is enabled via CsrfViewMiddleware in pygoat/settings.py:42-50, but several authenticated endpoints are reachable without a valid CSRF token because the application uses custom cookie-based auth (`auth_token`) and exposes unsafe state-changing actions via AJAX/non-form requests where token handling is missing or malformed.

Key vulnerable endpoints and evidence:

1) Admin use
